title: Dodge [Action]
excerpt: The Dodge action commits a creature's action to active defense, imposing disadvantage on attacks against it and improving Dexterity saving throws until its next turn begins.
markdown:
# Dodge [Action]

The Dodge action commits a creature's [[action]] to active defense. Until the start of that creature's next turn, [[attack_roll|attack rolls]] against it have [[disadvantage]], and the creature has [[advantage]] on Dexterity [[saving_throw|saving throws]].

## Core Effect

- Dodge represents a creature devoting its action to evasive movement, guarded posture, and threat awareness
- the action improves the creature's defense against direct attacks and effects that can be avoided with quick movement
- the benefit lasts until the start of the creature's next turn unless another rule prevents the creature from using it effectively

## Timing

Dodge protects the creature through the rest of the current round and into other creatures' turns, ending when that creature's next turn begins.

## Limits

- Dodge does not stop attacks from being made; it changes how those attacks are resolved
- Dodge does not grant extra movement, cover, or resistance by itself
- if the creature cannot actively respond to threats, the world state can prevent Dodge from providing its normal benefit
